# Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

pysphinx-mcp is built by and for people. As contributors and maintainers, we are
committed to ensuring that participation in this project remains a positive
experience for everyone regardless of race, ethnicity, caste, color, age, physical characteristics, neurodiversity, disability, sex or gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, language, philosophy or religion, national or social origin, socio-economic position, level of education, or other status.

## Our Standards

We expect all participants to help us maintain a respectful and constructive
environment. Behaviors that support this include:

- Choosing language that is welcoming and considerate
- Listening openly to perspectives different from your own
- Giving and receiving feedback with grace and good intent
- Owning your mistakes and learning from them
- Treating others with the same care you would want in return

Behaviors that will not be tolerated include:

- Sexual language, imagery, or unwanted advances of any kind
- Mockery, slurs, and attacks on a person's character or beliefs
- Sustained harassment, whether in public channels or private messages
- Disclosing someone's personal details without their consent
- Impersonating another person or evading moderation actions
- Any behavior that a reasonable person would consider inappropriate
  in a professional setting

## Maintainer Responsibilities

Maintainers set the tone for this community. They are responsible for
communicating expectations clearly and responding fairly to reports of
misconduct. This includes the authority to edit, remove, or reject any
contribution and to restrict or remove any participant whose behavior
is harmful to the project or its community.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct covers all spaces associated with pysphinx-mcp: the
repository, issue tracker, and any other official channel. It also applies
whenever someone is acting as a representative of the project in public,
for example: at an event, on social media, or via an official project account.

## Reporting & Enforcement

If you witness or experience behavior that violates this Code of Conduct,
please report it by opening a confidential issue or contacting the maintainer
directly at `vmphase.dev@gmail.com`. Reports will be reviewed promptly and handled
with confidentiality. Depending on the nature of the incident, responses may
range from a private warning to a temporary or permanent removal from the
project. Maintainers who fail to act in good faith are held to the same standard.

## Attribution

_This Code of Conduct is inspired by the
[Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 3.0._
